title: NiFi cluster is degraded
description: Alerts when a monitored NiFi instance reports that one or more cluster nodes have disconnected from the cluster coordinator (nifi.cluster.is_healthy = 0). This metric is emitted only in clustered mode, so the monitor ignores missing data and never fires for standalone deployments.

definition.name: [NiFi] Cluster degraded on {{host.name}}
definition.message:
{{#is_alert}}
## What's happening?

NiFi is reporting `nifi.cluster.is_healthy` = 0 (degraded) on {{host.name}} for the entire evaluation window: one or more nodes have disconnected from the cluster coordinator, so `nifi.cluster.connected_node_count` is below `nifi.cluster.total_node_count`. Current value: {{value}}.
{{/is_alert}}

{{#is_recovery}}
## Recovered

`nifi.cluster.is_healthy` on {{host.name}} has returned to 1—all nodes are reconnected to the cluster coordinator. Current value: {{value}}.
{{/is_recovery}}

{{^is_recovery}}

---

## Impact

Work is no longer balanced across the full cluster: FlowFiles owned by a disconnected node stop being processed until it rejoins, site-to-site and load-balanced connections rebalance onto the remaining nodes, and primary-node or coordinator-only tasks can be interrupted while the cluster re-elects.

---

## Why?

Possible causes:
* **Node lost contact with the coordinator**—a network partition, firewall or security-group change, or DNS issue between a node and the cluster coordinator or ZooKeeper.
* **JVM pause on a node**—a long garbage-collection pause (see `nifi.system.jvm.heap_utilization`) caused a node to miss cluster heartbeats and be marked disconnected.
* **Node process or host unhealthy**—the NiFi process crashed, ran out of heap, or the underlying host went down, dropping the node from the cluster.
* **Manual or coordinator-initiated disconnect**—an operator disconnected the node from the NiFi UI, or the coordinator evicted it after repeated missed heartbeats.
* **ZooKeeper quorum or coordinator election**—loss of ZooKeeper quorum or an in-progress coordinator election left nodes unable to stay registered.

---

## How to troubleshoot and resolve the issue?

1. **Identify the affected instance**—Note which `{{host.name}}` is alerting and compare `nifi.cluster.connected_node_count` against `nifi.cluster.total_node_count` in the [Metrics Explorer](/metric/explorer?exp_metric=nifi.cluster.connected_node_count&exp_scope=host:{{host.name}}&exp_agg=avg&exp_type=line) to see how many nodes dropped off.
2. **Open NiFi's Cluster management UI**—In NiFi, go to the top-right menu -> Cluster to see which node(s) report a `DISCONNECTED` state and the reason recorded by the coordinator.
3. **Check `nifi-app.log`**—On the disconnected node (and on the coordinator), review `nifi-app.log` around the alert time for heartbeat, disconnection, or ZooKeeper connection errors that identify the disconnected node(s).
4. **Restore and reconnect**—Once the root cause is fixed (connectivity, memory, process, or quorum), reconnect the node from the Cluster UI or restart the NiFi service on it, and confirm `nifi.cluster.connected_node_count` returns to `nifi.cluster.total_node_count`.

| Cause | Resolution |
| ----- | ---------- |
| Network partition / firewall | Restore connectivity to the coordinator and ZooKeeper; verify cluster and ZooKeeper ports are open. |
| Long JVM / GC pause | Tune heap and GC or raise the heartbeat interval; watch `nifi.system.jvm.heap_utilization`. |
| Node process crashed / host down | Investigate host health and restart NiFi on the affected node. |
| ZooKeeper quorum / election | Restore ZooKeeper quorum and confirm a coordinator is elected, then reconnect nodes. |

---

## Note on standalone deployments

`nifi.cluster.is_healthy` is only emitted by NiFi instances running in **clustered mode**. Standalone instances never emit this metric, so its absence is expected and normal—it is not a failure state. This monitor sets `on_missing_data` to `default`, so missing data never triggers an alert; it fires only on an actual value of 0 sustained across the window.

---

* [Logs](/logs?query=host:{{host.name}})
{{/is_recovery}}
